title: red lentil coconut curry
ingredients: 2 cup split red lentils (masoor dal); well rinsed and soaked for ~30 minutes with kombu if possible
1-2 cup cilantro; stems and leaves mixed is fine
2 14 oz can coconut milk; full fat
1 lemon; start with half and add to taste
instructions: Rinse and start soaking lentils (with a small piece of kombu if available)

Preheat saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion and fry for 8-10 minutes, sitting frequently, until soft and beginning to caramelize.  Add curry powder, pepper flakes (if using), garlic and ginger and fry for 2 more minutes, stirring continuously.
Add lentils and stir through for 1 minutes, then add tomatoes, cilantro stems, water, 2 tsp salt and a big grind of pepper.

Add the coconut milk. Bring to a boil then simmer for 25–45 minutes, until the lentils are soft but still holding a little shape. Fold in spinach and take off heat once fully wilted. Season with salt, sugar if needed and lemon juice.
Top with fresh cilantro if using
notes: I typically use a whole bin of spinach. By far, my favorite canned tomatoes are Bianco Dinapoli
